title: The Farmer and His Sons
lines: A Farmer, feeling his death approaching, called his sons to his bedside.
'My sons," said he, 'I am about to leave you all that I have.'
'But I will tell you the greatest treasure first: there is hidden gold in our fields.'
After his death, his sons began to dig in the fields, turning the soil over and over.
They found no gold, but the turned soil produced a bountiful harvest.
'Our father was wise," they said. 'He has given us more than gold.'
moral: Hard work is more valuable than hidden treasure.
